title: Active Page Indicator Styling on Settings Navigation
story: As a DataTools Pro user navigating Settings pages, I want the left navigation links to be visually distinct — green for clickable links and black for the current page — so that I have clear feedback about which page I am on.
description:
The Settings pages left navigation sidebar lacks visual feedback for the currently selected page. All links appear the same, making it unclear which page is active.

## Changes
- Navigation links styled in brand green to signal they are clickable
- The currently active page link changes from green to black to indicate selection
- Standard link CSS conventions applied consistently across all Settings sub-pages
acceptance_criteria: Left navigation links on Settings pages are displayed in brand green
The currently active Settings page link is displayed in black
Visual distinction is clear between clickable and selected navigation items
Styling is consistent across all Settings sub-pages
ux_changes: Applied brand green color to left navigation links on Settings pages. Active/current page link switches to black to provide visual feedback for the selected state.
implementation_notes: CSS changes applied to the Settings page left navigation to add active state indicator. Green for clickable links, black for selected page.
comment_summary: Developer implemented and pushed to staging. No revisions needed.
